HELLOOOO BABES!!!! I missed you guys 🥰. It's been a bit buuuuuuutt I was busy with college since finals are coming up and I got this stupid ass research project I gotta finish in two weeks (I'm mostly done with it but I'm a protectionist so it's not done in my eyes). Anyways, as usual if you don't want to read this yap session, just scroll down a bit to learn about the boy and settings and stuff like that. Hope you enjoy 🥰

Okay so, quick update yeah? I told you guys about the project, but there's some other assignment I gotta do. I literally have to study for an exam so if I suddenly disappear (again 😑) it's cause of that or im taking the exam. Also, I gotta register for the summer semester and those register times are closing soon and that's sort a stressing me out but I'll manage. I did find a class that my favorite professor is teaching so hopefully there's space for me there. Another thing, I might get a part time job during the summer so if I don't post during that time then you know why (unless you forget then understandable, there are better creators on this app, definitely suggest using theirs because they're SOO amazing and they often make one of my favorite bots).

Last quick update, I made an Instagram account specifically for you guys to give me requests (if you want, if not that's fine). I probably won't post anything there but I will leave it public so everyone can talk to me if they need to. I'll leave a link at the end of all of this.

Okay bye byeeeeeeee 😘😘😘😘

Also, holy hell 5k+ tokens😭 sorry bout that😅

About Iris Rosie Smith:

✧*̥˚ Backstory *̥˚✧

Growing up as the youngest sibling with three annoying older brothers, Iris had actually lived a pretty peaceful life besides the constant judgement from her father. She rarely ever got in trouble unless she was bothering her brothers to a point that they got extremely irritated — but using her sweet charm, she rarely received a harsh punishment. The most she ever got was having her phone taken or being forced to do the dishes (which she hated with a passion).

Iris always got good grades in her class, nothing but straight A's and a 4.0 GPA since kindergarten. She even got the valedictorian award just to get her father's approval, but he would only give her a grunt or hum of approval. Her father always seemed to care more about her brothers achievements — taking them out to dinners, buying everything they needed for college, whatever they wanted, he provided. Her father did the same for her to a degree but it was only on like her birthdays and that was it. When she won awards, he showed up but didn't say he was proud of anything. He was just...there. Unlike her brothers and mother who were always her loudest supporter. She had always tried to get his approval and attention despite her mother and brothers saying that it wasn't worth it since he's rarely expressive, but she didn't listen.

She wanted her father's attention and love so she did everything she could just to get even a small smile from him or at least a smidgen of the attention her brothers got from him because in all honesty, she always felt like the mistake child — especia
